what_it_does: Headless acceptance test for the fabric client retry/timeout layer. It injects fake transports and a recording sleep function to verify config-driven timeout defaults, environment override behavior, exponential+jittered backoff, final-attempt sleep skipping, retry-on-transient-error success, fail-loud behavior after default retries, and embedding dimension validation through the node.
observations: Uses subprocess to test env override because config reads the environment at import time.
Defines local fake transport factories (transport_of, embeddings_transport_of) that queue responses/exceptions.
Statistically samples _backoff to verify exponential growth, jitter variance, and cap.
Counts sleeps to prove _retry_sleep skips the final attempt.
Monkey-patches transport.openai_embeddings_transport to test embed.run dimension enforcement.
Expects default retries of 4 and specific config constants (180, 300, 1024).
No live network calls; entirely deterministic with injected fakes.
Uses a global ok accumulator in check() rather than a test framework.
standouts: Subprocess env-override test proving import-time config reads.
Sampling-based verification of jittered exponential backoff bounds.
Direct monkeypatching of the embeddings transport factory to test node-level dimension contract.
Fail-loud contract verification for all-empty responses and dimension mismatches.
conventions: Large explanatory module docstring describing the problem, fix, and test plan.
Dependency injection via fake functions instead of mocking framework.
Self-running __main__ script with custom PASS/FAIL reporting.
Restores monkey-patched transport in finally blocks.
concerns: Monkey-patches transport.openai_embeddings_transport directly; an unexpected exception path could leave the patch in place, though finally guards are present.
Hardcodes expected config constants (180, 300, 1024) in assertion labels, making the test brittle if config changes.
Statistical jitter test could theoretically flake, though it uses 50 samples.
Relies on a global ok variable rather than a structured test runner.
notes: A self-running acceptance script that documents and verifies fabric retry/backoff and timeout behavior using injected fakes instead of live endpoints.
questions: What are the exact implementations of _backoff, _retry_sleep, client.complete, and complete_embeddings?
How does the COMPANY_FABRIC_TIMEOUT env variable map to fcfg.DEFAULT_TIMEOUT?
What is the signature and behavior of nodes.embed.run?
Are DEFAULT_TIMEOUT, DEFAULT_CLOUD_TIMEOUT, and DEFAULT_EMBED_DIM defined exactly as the test expects?
Is there a real ollama-cloud transport that these tests are standing in for?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the docstring and the test code both focus on config-driven timeouts, exponential jittered backoff, skipping final-attempt sleep, fail-loud retries, and embedding dimension enforcement.
intention_signals: Make the retry/timeout behavior observable, configurable, and verifiable without a live ollama-cloud endpoint; ensure slow-but-progressing generations are not killed by a bare timeout and transient hard failures are retried with backoff.
novelty: Combines statistical sampling of jittered backoff bounds, a subprocess env-override test, and direct monkeypatching of the transport factory to test a node-level dimension contract in one self-contained acceptance script.
contribution: Provides a runnable, fake-transport acceptance test that documents and verifies the intended retry/timeout/dimension contracts for the fabric layer, which a fused system could keep as a behavioral regression guard.
summary_for_embedding: Self-contained headless acceptance test for fabric client retry/timeout and embedding dimension validation using injected fake transports and a recording sleep function.
intention_for_embedding: To prove the fabric layer handles ollama-cloud latency variance through config-driven timeouts, exponential jittered backoff, skip-final-sleep retries, and fail-loud dimension checks without live endpoints.

symbols.does: Test assertion helper that updates a global ok flag and prints a PASS/FAIL line for a labeled condition.
symbols.description: check(label, cond) evaluates a boolean condition, accumulates the result into the module-level ok variable, and prints a formatted PASS or FAIL line. It is the only assertion primitive used in this self-running acceptance script.

symbols.does: Returns a list and a closure that records every sleep duration requested by the retry logic.
symbols.description: recording_sleep() creates a calls list and a fake sleep function that appends each requested delay to calls. This allows deterministic verification of how many sleeps occur and whether backoff delays grow as expected.

symbols.does: Factory that builds a fake chat transport returning queued responses or raising queued exceptions in order.
symbols.description: transport_of(*responses) returns a function t that consumes an iterator of queued responses or exceptions. Each call to t pops the next item, raising it if it is an exception or returning it otherwise, enabling controlled simulation of transient chat transport failures.

symbols.does: Inner fake chat transport produced by transport_of.
symbols.description: t(model, messages, **opts) is the closure returned by transport_of. It advances the queued iterator and either raises the next exception or returns the next response, ignoring the chat request arguments.

symbols.does: Factory that builds a fake embeddings transport returning queued vector lists or raising queued exceptions in order.
symbols.description: embeddings_transport_of(*responses) returns a function t that consumes an iterator of queued embedding vectors or exceptions. Each call pops the next item, raising it if it is an exception or returning it otherwise, enabling controlled simulation of embeddings transport behavior.

symbols.does: Inner fake embeddings transport produced by embeddings_transport_of.
symbols.description: t(model, inputs) is the closure returned by embeddings_transport_of. It advances the queued iterator and either raises the next exception or returns the next vector list, ignoring the embeddings request arguments.

symbols.does: Entry point that runs all acceptance checks for timeout, retry, and embedding dimension behavior.
symbols.description: main() executes the full acceptance suite: it checks config-driven timeout defaults, verifies an env override via subprocess, samples _backoff behavior, counts sleeps to prove final-attempt skipping, tests transient-error recovery and fail-loud empty responses, and validates embedding dimension enforcement through the node. It returns 0 on full success and 1 otherwise.

symbols.does: Local fake chat transport inside main that counts attempts before returning empty.
symbols.description: counting_empty(model, messages, **opts) increments attempts_seen['n'] and returns an empty string on every call. It is used to verify that client.complete() makes exactly the default number of retries before raising FabricError.
